text: Whole blood samples from mice were aliquoted into EDTA-coated tubes (0.5 M, 20 μl) and subjected to 1,000 × g centrifugation for 10 min at 4°C. The resulting supernatant was harvested as plasma fraction for subsequent analysis. Plasma amylase activity was quantified colorimetrically via ethylphenoxy-polysaccharide hydrolysis (EPS method), while lipase activity was determined enzymatically through methyltriazine chromogenic reaction, both measurements conducted using a Beckman AU1000 clinical analyzer.
